content.text:
[cron:cf640af1-ecb5-4b6c-9a24-d8f058a323c2 hourly-memory-summary] Hourly memory summary job:
1. Get all chat messages from last 60 minutes: both this DM and the main group
2. Extract: Hard facts, decisions, requests, bugs, changes, agreements, Mood, tone, emotional state, vibe, frustrations, satisfaction, Unspoken context, things implied between lines, Preferences, likes, dislikes that were shown not stated, Trust signals, jokes that landed, things that annoyed
3. Do NOT discard banter and casual talk - this is where most of the context lives.
4. Summarize cleanly, separate Facts section and Context / Tone section
5. Append this summary cleanly to today's daily memory file
6. No notifications required. Run silently.
Current time: Sunday, April 12th, 2026 - 2:34 PM (UTC) / 2026-04-12 14:34 UTC

Return your summary as plain text; it will be delivered automatically. If the task explicitly calls for messaging a specific external recipient, note who/where it should go instead of sending it yourself.

content.thinking: To get the last 60 minutes of session history, I will check both main and group sessions.Listing sessions to get session keys comes first, followed by checking the date memory file and recent messages.
First, I'll pull recent message history and check the memory file date.Today's date is confirmed as 2026-04-12 UTC, IST shares the same date.

arguments.command: mkdir -p memory && date +"%Y-%m-%d" && ls -la memory/ 2>/dev/null || echo "memory dir created"

content.thinking: I will first fetch the last hour's session history to extract relevant information.Will compile summary and append to daily memory file.

arguments.command: cd /root/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/ && ls -lt --time-style=+%s *.jsonl | head -15 | awk '{print $7 " " $6}' | while read f t; do if [ $t -gt $((`date +%s` - 3600)) ]; then echo "== $f =="; jq -r 'select(.role=="user" or .role=="assistant") | "[\(.timestamp | todate)] \(.role): \(.content)"' $f | tail -30; fi; done
